{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kumihama-chō|Q11369657}}, {{jalink|Kyōtango|Q513897}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{jalink|Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{jalink|Kumano District|Q11568982}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}.

During the {{jalink|Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{jalink|Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{jalink|Tango Peninsula|Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{jalink|Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Mishimada Shrine
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=京丹後市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyōtango|Q513897}}{{jalink|Kumihama-chō|Q11369657}} Kanaya Imagota 791
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
|社格   = former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
|創建   = 
|本殿   = Sangen-sha
|別名   = Mishima Daimyōjin
|札所等 =
|例祭   = September 9<ref name="特選神名牒"/>
|神事   =
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mishimada Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kumihama-chō|Q11369657}}, {{jalink|Kyōtango|Q513897}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{jalink|Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{jalink|Kumano District|Q11568982}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}.

During the {{jalink|Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{jalink|Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{jalink|Tango Peninsula|Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{jalink|Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kumihama-chō|Q11369657}}, {{jalink|Kyōtango|Q513897}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{jalink|Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{jalink|Kumano District|Q11568982}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}.

During the {{jalink|Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{jalink|Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{jalink|Tango Peninsula|Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{jalink|Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Mishimada Shrine
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=京丹後市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kyōtango|Q513897}}{{jalink|Kumihama-chō|Q11369657}} Kanaya Imagota 791
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}
|社格   = former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
|創建   = 
|本殿   = Sangen-sha
|別名   = Mishima Daimyōjin
|札所等 =
|例祭   = September 9<ref name="特選神名牒"/>
|神事   =
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mishimada Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kumihama-chō|Q11369657}}, {{jalink|Kyōtango|Q513897}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{jalink|Emperor Kōrei|Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{jalink|Fudoki|Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{jalink|Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{jalink|Kumano District|Q11568982}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}.

During the {{jalink|Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{jalink|Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{jalink|Tango Peninsula|Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{jalink|Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== Notes ===

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

== Japanese content ==

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Mishimada Shrine
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=京丹後市}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897}}{{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657}} Kanaya Imagota 791
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Mishimada Shrine
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=京丹後市}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}{{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}} Kanaya Imagota 791
|位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
|社格   = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. {{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== Notes ===

== Japanese content ==

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

{{画像提供依頼|date=2024年4月|cat=京丹後市|Carvings of the honden by Nakaigonji}}
'''Mishimada Shrine''' (みしまだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumihama-chō|Q11369657|WD=Q11369657|1=Kumihama}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōtango|Q513897|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is identified with Mishimata Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref name="特選神名牒">Ministry of Religion, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/337 Tokusen Jinmyōchō], Isobe Kōyōdō, 1925, p.603.</ref>. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its rank was former gōsha.

Its honden is designated a cultural property by Kyōtango City<ref name="デジタルミュージアム">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/2934.html Digital Museum C6 Mishimada Shrine] Kyōtango City.</ref>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
; Main deity
* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}<ref name="特選神名牒"/>

=== History ===
In the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōrei|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, Take-morosumi-no-Mikoto is said to have enshrined Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, Uetsuwatatsumi-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto at Ikushima in Kawakami valley and named it Mishima Shrine<ref name=新神社検索>[https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinja-all/jsearch3all.php?jinjya=4812 Mishimada Shrine] Shin-jinja Search.</ref>. Another tradition, according to ''Tango Kujiki'', says that in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Kawakami Masu invited it, as written in the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}}<ref name=新神社検索/>. The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

{{nihongo|'''Mishimada Shrine'''|三嶋田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tango Province]].

The {{ill|lt=Tango no Kuni Fudoki|Q11368517|WD=Q11368517|1=Tango no Kuni Fudoki}} itself is lost and does not survive.

It is identified with the shikinaisha small shrine Mishimata Shrine in {{ill|lt=Kumano District|Q11568982|WD=Q11568982|1=Kumano district}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.

During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it was protected by {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and afterwards prospered under the reverence of the Nomura clan<ref>[https://tanba.jp/2016/02/%E4%B8%89%E5%B6%8B%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88%E4%BA%AC%E4%B8%B9%E5%BE%8C%E5%B8%82%E4%B9%85%E7%BE%8E%E6%B5%9C%E7%94%BA%E9%87%91%E8%B0%B7%EF%BC%89-2/ Mishimada Shrine (Kanaya, Kumihama-chō, Kyōtango)] Tanba Shimbun.</ref>.

In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Mishimata Daimyōjin<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. In Tenpō 13 (1842), shrine carpenters of {{ill|lt=Kanaya Village (Kyoto)|Q35797263|WD=Q35797263|lt=Kanaya Village|1=Kanaya}} built the honden<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>.

Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was first designated village shrine, then promoted to gōsha in 1875 (Meiji 8)<ref name=新神社検索/>.

=== Cultural properties ===
=== City-designated cultural properties ===
* Mishimada Shrine honden
** Built in Tenpō 13 (1842)<ref name="市指定文化財">[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3326.html City-designated cultural properties in Kyōtango] Kyōtango City.</ref>. A relatively rare sangen-sha, regarded as one of the finest honden on the {{ill|lt=Tango Peninsula|Q10877313|WD=Q10877313|1=Tango Peninsula}}<ref name="デジタルミュージアム"/>. The carvings are by Nakaigonji Masasada, 6th-generation master of the {{ill|lt=Nakaigonji lineage|Q20041763|WD=Q20041763|1=Nakaigonji lineage}}. Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

* Mishimada Shrine stone statues (standing Jizō Bosatsu, standing Shaka Nyorai, standing Shō-Kanzeon Bosatsu)
** The Jizō and Kanzeon statues are Muromachi works, and the Shaka statue has an inscription dated Eikyō 12 (1440)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>. They are among the few signed mid-Muromachi stone works in Tango<ref>[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3/13641.html Mishimada Shrine stone statues] Kyōtango City.</ref>. Designated September 1, 2020 (Reiwa 2)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.

=== City-designated cultural-property environmental conservation district ===
* Mishimada Shrine environmental conservation district
** Designated July 15, 1991 (Heisei 3)<ref name="市指定文化財"/>.
